question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2024

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five Sections - A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A, Questions no. 1 to 18 are multiple choice questions (MCQs) and questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B, Questions no. 21 to 25 are very short answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C, Questions no. 26 to 31 are short answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D, Questions no. 32 to 35 are long answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E, Questions no. 36 to 38 are case study based questions carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 3 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A

This section comprises multiple choice questions (MCQs) of 1 mark each.
1.

Let {tex}\theta{/tex} be the angle between two unit vectors {tex}\hat {a}{/tex} and {tex}\hat {b}{/tex} such that sin {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}. Then, {tex}\hat {a}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\hat {b}{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex} \pm \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
b. {tex} \pm \frac{3}{4}{/tex}
c. {tex} \pm \frac{4}{5}{/tex}
d. {tex} \pm \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
2.

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y=x^4-3 x{/tex} is:

a. x
b. -x
c. x-1
d. {tex} \log \left(x^{-1}\right){/tex}
3.

If the direction cosines of a line are {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}k, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}k, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}k, then the value of k is:

a. {tex}\pm{/tex}1
b. {tex}\pm{/tex}{tex}\sqrt3{/tex}
c. {tex}\pm{/tex}3
d. {tex}\pm{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
4.

A linear programming problem deals with the optimization of a/an:

a. logarithmic function
b. linear function
c. quadratic function
d. exponential function
5.

If {tex}\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{B})=\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A}^{\prime} \mid \mathrm{B}\right){/tex}, then which of the following statements is true?

a. {tex}\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A})=\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A}^{\prime}\right){/tex}
b. P(A) = 2 P(B)
c. {tex} {P}({A} \cap {B})=\frac{1}{2} {P}({B}){/tex}
d. {tex} {P}({A} \cap {B})=2 {P}({B}){/tex}
6.

If aij and Aij represent the (ij)th element and its cofactor of {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} respectively, then the value of a11 A21 + a12 A22 + a13 A23 is:

a. 0
b. -28
c. 114
d. -114
7.

The derivative of sin (x2) w.r.t. x, at x = {tex}\sqrt{\pi}{/tex} is:

a. 1
b. -1
c. -2{tex}\sqrt{\pi}{/tex}
d. 2{tex}\sqrt{\pi}{/tex}
8.

The order and degree of the differential equation {tex}\left[1+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}\right]=\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} respectively, are

a. 1, 2
b. 2, 2
c. 2, 1
d. 4, 2
9.

The vector with terminal point A (2, -3, 5) and initial point B (3, -4, 7) is:

a. {tex} \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
b. {tex} \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
c. {tex} -\hat{i}-\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
d. {tex} -\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
10.

The distance of point P(a, b, c) from y-axis is:

a. b
b. b2
c. {tex} \sqrt{a^2+c^2}{/tex}
d. a2 + c2
11.

The number of corner points of the feasible region determined by constraints x {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, x + y {tex}\geq{/tex} 4 is:

a. 0
b. 1
c. 2
d. 3
12.

If A and B are two non-zero square matrices of same order such that (A + B)2 = A2 + B2, then:

a. AB = O
b. AB = - BA
c. BA = O
d. AB = BA
13.

A relation R defined on set A = {tex}\{x: x \in Z \text { and } 0 \leq x \leq 10\} \text { as } R=\{(x, y): x=y\}{/tex} is given to be an equivalence relation. The number of equivalence classes is:

a. 1
b. 2
c. 10
d. 11
14.

If a matrix has 36 elements, the number of possible orders it can have, is:

a. 13
b. 3
c. 5
d. 9
15.

The number of points, where {tex}\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=[\mathrm{x}], 0<\mathrm{x}<3([\cdot]{/tex} denotes greatest integer function) is not differentiable is:

a. 1
b. 2
c. 3
d. 4
16.

Let f(x) be a continuous function on [a, b] and differentiable on (a, b). Then, this function f(x) is strictly increasing in (a, b) if

a. {tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})<0, \forall {x} \in({a}, {b}){/tex}
b. {tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})>0, \forall {x} \in({a}, {b}){/tex}
c. {tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})=0, \forall {x} \in({a}, {b}){/tex}
d. {tex}f(x)>0, \forall x \in(a, b){/tex}
17.

If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}{x}+{y} & 2 \\ 5 & {xy}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}6 & 2 \\ 5 & 8\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\left(\frac{24}{x}+\frac{24}{y}\right){/tex} is:

a. 7
b. 6
c. 8
d. 18
18.

If {tex}\int_\limits0^{\mathrm{a}} \frac{1}{4+\mathrm{x}^2} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} , then the value of ‘a’ is:

a. {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}
b. {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
c. {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}

Questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion and Reason based questions. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and the other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not
    the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
19.

Assertion (A): A line in space cannot be drawn perpendicular to x, y and z axes simultaneously.
Reason (R): For any line making angles, {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\gamma{/tex} with the positive directions of x, y and z axes respectively, cos2{tex}\alpha{/tex} + cos2{tex}\beta{/tex} + cos2{tex}\gamma{/tex} = 1.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
20.

Assertion (A): For matrix A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & \cos \theta & 1 \\ -\cos \theta & 1 & \cos \theta \\ -1 & -\cos \theta & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, where {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\in{/tex} [0, 2{tex}\pi{/tex}], |A| {tex}\in{/tex}[2, 4].
Reason (R): cos {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\in{/tex}[-1, 1], {tex} \forall \theta \in{/tex} [0, 2{tex}\pi{/tex}].

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

SECTION B

This section comprises very short answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{x} \sqrt{1+2 \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex} \int_0^{\frac{\pi^2}{4}} \frac{\sin \sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex}
22.

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are two non-zero vectors such that {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \perp \vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}) \perp \vec{b}{/tex}, then prove that {tex}|\vec{b}|=\sqrt{2}|\vec{a}|{/tex}.
23.

In the given figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. If {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{D B}=3 \hat{i}-6 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}\overrightarrow{A D}{/tex} and hence find the area of parallelogram ABCD.
24.(i)

If {tex} y=\sqrt{\cos x+y} {/tex} prove that {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin x}{1-2 y} \text {. }{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Show that the function f(x) = |x|3 is differentiable at all points of its domain.
25.

Find the absolute maximum and minimum values of the function {tex}f(x)=12 x^{4 / 3}-6 x^{1 / 3}, x \in[0,1]{/tex}

SECTION C

This section comprises short answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{x^2}{\left(x^2+4\right)\left(x^2+9\right)} d x {/tex}

OR
(ii)

Evaluate : {tex}\int_1^3(|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-3|) d x {/tex}
27.

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{2 x y}{/tex}.
28.

Solve the following linear programming problem graphically:
Maximise z = 5x + 4y
subject to the constraints
{tex} x+2 y \geq 4 {/tex}
{tex} 3 x+y \leq 6 {/tex}
{tex} x+y \leq 4 {/tex}
{tex} x, y \geq 0 {/tex}
29.

E and F are two independent events such that P({tex}\bar{E}{/tex}) = 0.6 and P(E {tex}\cup{/tex} F) = 0.6. Find P(F) and P({tex}\bar{E}{/tex} {tex}\cup{/tex} {tex}\bar{F}{/tex}).
30.(i)

A relation R on set A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} is defined as R = {(x, y) : |x2 - y2| < 8. Check whether the relation R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive.

OR
(ii)

A function f is defined from R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R as f(x) = ax + b, such that f(1) = 1 and f(2) = 3. Find function f(x). Hence, check whether function f(x) is one-one and onto or not.
31.(i)

If {tex}\sqrt{1-x^2}+\sqrt{1-y^2}{/tex} = a(x - y), then prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{1-y^2}}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}.

OR
(ii)

If y = (tan x)x, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

SECTION D

This section comprises long answer type questions (LA) of 5 marks each.
32.(i)

Evaluate : {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} e^x\left(\frac{1+\sin x}{1+\cos x}\right) d x{/tex}

OR
(ii)

{tex}\int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\sqrt {\sin 2x} }}dx} {/tex}
33.

Using intergration find the area of the ellipse {tex}\frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1{/tex}, included between the lines x = -2 and x = 2.
34.

Equations of sides of a parallelogram ABCD are as follows:
{tex}\mathrm{AB}: \quad \frac{\mathrm{x}+1}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-2}{-2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}B C: \quad \frac{x-1}{3}=\frac{y+2}{-5}=\frac{z-5}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{CD}: \frac{\mathrm{x}-4}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}+7}{-2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-8}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\text { DA: } \frac{x-2}{3}=\frac{y+3}{-5}=\frac{z-4}{3}{/tex}
Find the equation of diagonal BD.
35.(i)

If A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of equations:
x - 2y = 10, 2x - y - z = 8, -2y + z = 7

OR
(ii)

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -1 & \mathrm{a} & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & \mathrm{x} \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A-1 = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ -8 & 7 & -5 \\ {~b} & {y} & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find the value of (a + x) - (b + y).

SECTION E

This section comprises 3 case study based questions of 4 marks each.

Case Study - 1
36.

Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
According to recent research, air turbulence has increased in various regions around the world due to climate change. Turbulence makes flights bumpy and often delays the flights.
Assume that, an airplane observes severe turbulence, moderate turbulence or light turbulence with equal probabilities. Further, the chance of an airplane reaching late to the destination are 55%, 37% and 17% due to severe, moderate and light turbulence respectively.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Find the probability that an airplane reached its destination late. (2)
  2. If the airplane reached its destination late, find the probability that it was due to moderate turbulence. (2)

Case Study - 2
37.

Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
If a function f : X {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Y defined as f(x) = y is one-one and onto, then we can define a unique function g : Y {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} X such that g(y) = x, where x {tex}\in{/tex} X and y = f(x), y {tex}\in{/tex} Y. Function g is called the inverse of function f.
The domain of sine function is R and function sine : R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R is neither one-one nor onto. The following graph shows the sine function.

Let sine function be defined from set A to [-1, 1] such that inverse of sine function exists, i.e., sin-1 x is defined from [-1, 1] to A.
On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. If A is the interval other than principal value branch, give an example of one such interval. (1)
  2. If sin-1 (x) is defined from [-1, 1] to its principal value branch, find the value of sin-1 {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} - sin-1 (1). (1)
    1. Draw the graph of sin-1 x from [-1, 1] to its principal value branch. (2)
      OR
    2. Find the domain and range of f(x) = 2 sin-1 (1 - x). (2)

Case Study - 3
38.

Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
The traffic police has installed Over Speed Violation Detection (OSVD) system at various locations in a city. These cameras can capture a speeding vehicle from a distance of 300 m and even function in the dark.

A camera is installed on a pole at the height of 5 m. It detects a car travelling away from the pole at the speed of 20 m/s. At any point, x m away from the base of the pole, the angle of elevation of the speed camera from the car C is {tex}\theta{/tex}.
On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Express {tex}\theta{/tex} in terms of height of the camera installed on the pole and x. (1)
  2. Find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d} \theta}{\mathrm{dx}} {/tex}. (1)
    1. Find the rate of change of angle of elevation with respect to time at an instant when the car is 50 m away from the pole. (2)
      OR
    2. If the rate of change of angle of elevation with respect to time
      of another car at a distance of 50 m from the base of the pole is {tex}\frac{3}{101}{/tex} rad/s, then find the speed of the car. (2)

A relation R defined on set A = {tex}\{x: x \in Z \text { and } 0 \leq x \leq 10\} \text { as } R=\{(x, y): x=y\}{/tex} is given to be an equivalence relation. The number of equivalence classes is:

Explanation:

The number of equivalence classes for the relation R defined on the set A={x:x∈Z and 0≤x≤10} with the property that x=y is the same as the number of distinct elements in the set A. If {tex}\int_\limits0^{\mathrm{a}} \frac{1}{4+\mathrm{x}^2} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} , then the value of ‘a’ is:

b. {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
c. {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
Correct Answer: (b) {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
Explanation:

The given integral is:
{tex}\int_\limits0^{\mathrm{a}} \frac{1}{4+\mathrm{x}^2} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
To solve this integral, we can use the substitution x = 2 tan {tex}\theta{/tex}. Then, dx = 2 sec2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex}.
Substituting these into the integral, we get:
{tex} \int_0^a \frac{1}{4+x^2} d x=\int_0^{\arctan (a / 2)} \frac{2 \sec ^2 \theta}{4+4 \tan ^2 \theta} d \theta{/tex}
Simplifying the expression inside the integral:
{tex} \int_0^{\arctan (a / 2)} \frac{1}{2} d \theta=\frac{1}{2}[\theta]_0^{\arctan (a / 2)}{/tex}
Evaluating the integral at the limits of integration:
{tex} \frac{1}{2}[\arctan (a / 2)-0]=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
Solving for a:
{tex} \arctan (a / 2)=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}
Taking the tangent of both sides:
{tex} a / 2=\tan (\pi / 3)=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}a=2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}.
